payload.title: rd join: multi-root beacon resolution and failover on name-resolution failure

payload.context:
Follow-up from ready-9df. Design ref: federation-auth-naming-v2.md line 460. TOFU pinning is done (applyBeaconRootTOFU in join.go). Remaining: (1) when rd join resolves a name via beacon, try multiple roots if the first fails. (2) On name-resolution failure, surface clear error with retry guidance. Currently join.go loads a single operator root (line 315). Needs: try configured roots in order, failover to next on timeout/error.

## Done condition
rd join with a name that requires beacon resolution tries all configured roots in order. If the first root times out or errors, the next root is tried. If all roots fail, a clear error message lists which roots were tried and suggests --confirm to override TOFU pin.

## File paths
- cmd/rd/join.go — resolveName, applyBeaconRootTOFU
- pkg/rdconfig/config.go — BeaconRoot (may need BeaconRoots plural)

## Agent type: implementer
## Model tier: sonnet
## Test-depth: feature

## Goal
E2e smoke test proving rd commands work when the user's identity lives at the legacy ~/.campfire/ path (pre-0.16 migration).

## Done condition
An e2e test that:
1. Creates a temporary CF_HOME at a path ending in .campfire/ (simulating legacy layout)
2. Runs cf init in that home to create an identity
3. Runs rd init --name testproject with CF_HOME pointing to the legacy path
4. Creates an item, lists items, shows item — all succeed
5. Verifies no errors about missing identity or wrong path

## File paths
- test/e2e/ — new test or extend existing e2e tests
- cmd/rd/root.go — identity resolution (verify it handles legacy path)

## Veracity constraint
Must use real cf init and real rd commands, not mocks.

## Agent type: implementer
## Model tier: haiku

Must use real cf init and real rd commands, not mocks.

payload.context: In cmd/rd/join.go, the beacon root is pinned to config (rdconfig.Save) BEFORE client.Join() is called. If the join fails (wrong campfire ID, transport error), the beacon root is permanently pinned to an untrusted value. The user must then explicitly run 'rd join --reset-beacon-root' to recover. Fix: move TOFU pin save to AFTER a successful join, or roll back on join failure. Severity: major.
payload.title: fix: TOFU beacon root pinned before join succeeds — spurious pin on failed join

payload.context: In cmd/rd/join.go, when stdin is not a terminal (CI pipelines, agent invocations), the TOFU first-use guard auto-pins any supplied --root value without user confirmation. A script that invokes 'rd join --root <attacker-controlled-id>' will silently pin a malicious beacon root. Subsequent joins using the correct root require --confirm or --reset-beacon-root to recover. Fix options: (1) require explicit --confirm flag even in non-interactive mode for first TOFU pin, (2) refuse to auto-pin in non-interactive mode and exit with a clear error, (3) document clearly that --confirm is required in CI. Severity: major — the whole point of TOFU is to surface first-use so users can verify the root.
payload.title: fix: TOFU non-interactive auto-pin silently accepts any beacon root in CI/agent context

payload.context: In cmd/rd/join.go + pkg/rdconfig/config.go, there is no file lock between Load() and Save(). Two concurrent 'rd join --root X' and 'rd join --root Y' processes can both read BeaconRoot='' simultaneously, both believe they are performing the first TOFU pin, and both write their respective roots — last writer wins silently. The expected behavior is that the second concurrent first-use should see a non-empty BeaconRoot and trigger the deviation guard. Fix: use file locking (e.g. flock or advisory lock on rd.json) around the Load-check-Save sequence in join, OR use an atomic compare-and-swap via OS-level rename semantics. Severity: minor in practice (concurrent rd join is rare) but is a logical integrity hole in the TOFU protocol.
payload.title: fix: TOCTOU race on TOFU beacon root pin — concurrent rd join can silently overwrite

payload.context: In pkg/state/state.go Derive(), when a cross-campfire ref appears as the BLOCKED item (p.BlockedID is cross-campfire, p.BlockerID is local), the code correctly attaches the warning to the local item (localItemID = p.BlockerID). However the warning message always uses p.BlockerID as the embedded ref string, not the actual cross-campfire ref (p.BlockedID). Result: the CrossCampfireWarning for this case says 'unresolved cross-campfire dep: <local-item-id> ...' — which is a local ID, not a cross-campfire ref. When crossdep.ResolveDeps processes this warning, extractRef returns the local item ID, ParseCrossCampfireRef returns nil (no dots), and the dep is returned as unresolvable with the wrong ref. This produces confusing output and means this dep relationship is never resolvable even when the user is a member of the relevant campfire. Fix: use p.BlockedID in the warning format string when blockedIsCross=true. Severity: minor (incorrect warning/display, not a security vulnerability).
payload.title: fix: cross-campfire warning message uses wrong item ID when blockedIsCross=true

payload.context: In pkg/crossdep/crossdep.go resolveSingle(), when full alias lookup on 'acme.frontend' fails, the code retries with only the last segment 'frontend'. If the user has a short alias 'frontend' pointing to a different campfire than the one the cross-campfire ref author intended, the dep resolves against the wrong campfire. This means a crafted work:block message with 'evil.frontend.item-abc' will resolve against whatever campfire the user has aliased as 'frontend', not 'evil.frontend'. An attacker who can send messages to a shared campfire and knows the user's alias names can probe which aliases exist (an alias hit vs miss produces different display output). The fallback was added for usability but weakens the namespace isolation that the dot-separated campfire name path is meant to provide. Fix: remove the last-segment fallback, or at minimum add a warning when the fallback is used. Severity: major — breaks namespace isolation guarantees.
payload.title: fix: last-segment alias fallback in crossdep may resolve unintended campfire

payload.context: In cmd/rd/join.go, neither campfireID (args[0]) nor beaconRoot (--root flag) are validated before use. Both are stored directly to config and passed to client.Join(). While the current uses are comparison and truncated display (no injection risk), storing arbitrary strings as campfire IDs in the config creates risk for future code that might use them in paths or protocol operations. Recommended: validate that both values match the expected hex ID format (64 hex characters) before proceeding. Severity: minor — no exploitable injection currently, but hygiene issue.
payload.title: fix: no input validation on campfireID or beaconRoot in rd join

payload.reason: Reviewed Wave 4 commit 6a9a0f4. 0 critical, 3 major, 3 minor findings. Child items created: ready-f43 (pin-before-join ordering — major), ready-a6c (non-interactive TOFU auto-pin bypass — major), ready-134 (last-segment alias fallback breaks namespace isolation — major), ready-2dc (TOCTOU race on TOFU pin — minor), ready-2dc (TOCTOU — minor), ready-129 (wrong item ID in cross-campfire warning — minor), ready-124 (no input validation on join args — minor). No credential leaks, no auth bypass in membership check, no injection surfaces. BeaconRoot stored at 0600 permissions. Membership check in crossdep correctly gates on store membership. Config save uses 0600 permissions. Primary concerns are TOFU protocol integrity (pin-before-join, non-interactive auto-pin, TOCTOU) and alias namespace isolation (last-segment fallback).

Reviewed Wave 4 tests: all 6 done conditions covered, 100+ tests passing, ground-source testing confirmed. No critical or major findings. Minor gap: no integration test for rd show displaying cross-campfire deps (but code path verified by inspection).
Code review complete: 0 critical, 2 major (DRY violations in ID truncation), 3 minor
Spec-compliance review: 1 critical (beacon root pins to rd.json not .cf/config.toml), 5/6 done conditions verified correct
Waves 1-4 implementation complete. Wave 4 (ready-9df) committed. Review findings filed as separate items.

Wave 1.5 local name binding shipped — existing tests pass
Wave 2 authorization layer shipped — org-observer e2e tests pass

payload.title: BUG: IssueToken panics on empty operations slice (index out of range)
BUG: IssueToken panics on empty operations slice (index out of range)
BUG: serverBindingPayload type mismatch between state and conventionserver breaks authorization gating
BUG: buildDepTree deletes visited entry after processing, allowing infinite recursion in cycle
BUG: validateNameFormat misses trailing /.. path traversal sequences
BUG: grantMsgID and result.MessageID truncated at [:12] without length guard — panic if short

payload.reason: Duplicate — superseded by ready-f34 which has the correct title for the captoken IssueToken panic bug

payload.context: pkg/captoken/captoken.go:188 — IssueToken() accesses operations[0] at line 188 before the len(operations)==0 guard at line 189. If called with an empty slice, this panics with index out of range. The len guard must be checked first. Trigger: any caller passing an empty operations list to IssueToken(). This is a latent panic in the capability token issuance path.

The serverBindingPayload struct is defined twice with incompatible field types: (1) conventionserver/server.go:410 — ValidFrom int64, ValidUntil int64 (writes JSON numbers). (2) state/state.go:206 — ValidFrom string, ValidUntil string (tries to unmarshal into strings). JSON numbers do NOT unmarshal into Go string fields — the field silently stays empty string. parseTimestamp('') returns 0. Result: findActiveServerBinding always returns nil because validFrom==0 causes every binding to be skipped as 'not yet valid'. This breaks isOperationAuthorized for ALL consequential operations (close, delegate, gate-resolve) in a campfire that has a server-binding declared — they are all rejected as unauthorized. Observable failure: work:close messages are silently dropped from derived state when a server-binding exists.

cmd/rd/dep.go:264 — buildDepTree() sets visited[id]=true (line 248), processes children (recursively calling buildDepTree with same visited map), then calls delete(visited, id) at line 264. The delete is meant to allow the same node to appear in multiple branches (not truly a cycle). However, if there IS a genuine cycle (A blocks B, B blocks A), the delete means: when processing A we mark visited[A]=true, descend into B, descend back into A — A IS in visited so we return the cycle node. But after B finishes and we delete visited[B], if A appears again via a different path, we'd recurse again. In a true cycle A->B->A, the delete at the end of A's processing means if A is reached via a third path, visited[A] is gone and we recurse again — infinite recursion/stack overflow. Trigger: item with cyclic block edges (A blocks B, B blocks A) plus a third path to A.

payload.reason: Dead code sweep complete: 32 unused items found (6 from staticcheck, 26 unreachable functions). All items identified as safe-to-delete. See item notes for full list.

payload.context: cmd/rd/join.go:278 — validateNameFormat() checks for path traversal via strings.Contains(input, '../') and input=='..'. But it misses the trailing case: 'foo/..' passes validation because it doesn't contain '../' (no trailing slash) and is not exactly '..'. An attacker or buggy name resolution returning 'malicious/..' would pass the format check. The fix: also check strings.HasSuffix(input, '/..'). Severity: medium — defense in depth issue since the validated value is used as a campfire name argument, not a filesystem path directly. But validateNameFormat is designed as a security gate, and having a gap undermines its purpose.

cmd/rd/admit.go:222,233 and cmd/rd/revoke.go:78,117 — multiple sites truncate message IDs or pubkeys with [:12] without a length guard. In admit.go lines 222 and 233: grantMsgID[:12] is called on the message ID returned by admitThenGrant. If the executor returns an ID shorter than 12 chars (unusual but possible in test doubles, error paths, or future protocol changes), this panics. Same pattern in revoke.go:78 result.MessageID[:12] and line 117 r2.MessageID[:12]. The display-only truncations for displayKey are guarded by a prior length check, but the grantMsgID/result.MessageID truncations are not. Fix: use min(12, len(id)) or check len before slicing, consistent with the minInt helper already defined in send.go.

payload.title: SEC: IssueToken panics on empty operations slice (out-of-bounds before nil check)
SEC: revoke uses campfireID as pubkey when target is a name (semantic type confusion)
SEC: IsSoloMode does not check server-binding ValidUntil — stale binding blocks solo mode indefinitely

payload.reason: Security sweep complete: 0 critical, 3 high, 4 medium, 1 low. High: (1) IssueToken panics on empty operations (captoken.go:188, operations[0] before len check) → ready-e11; (2) revoke resolves name to campfireID and uses it as pubkey in role-grant (revoke.go:45-49) → ready-34d; (3) IsSoloMode never checks ValidUntil on server-binding, stale binding blocks solo mode (server.go:448-468) → ready-7c3. Medium: (4) isHexString in inbox_watcher rejects uppercase hex, isHex in join.go accepts it — pubkey with uppercase chars passes admit but fails inbox; (5) requested_role field not validated before materializing to project campfire; (6) OptionalAttestations/OptionalJoinConversationCampfire have no length limits; (7) validateNameFormat misses trailing foo/.. pattern. Low: (8) authorizationMatrix defined but never enforced in makeHandler (design-correct for solo mode but misleading).

payload.title: BUG: role-grant granted_at sent as RFC3339 string but Derive() parses as int64 nanoseconds — field silently ignored

payload.context: cmd/rd/admit.go:274,278 and cmd/rd/revoke.go:63,68 — granted_at is built as RFC3339 string: now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339). It is passed in the role-grant payload as a string. In state/state.go:473, Derive() parses role-grant messages into a struct with 'GrantedAt int64'. JSON unmarshal of a string value into int64 fails silently in Go — the field stays 0. So p.GrantedAt==0 always, and the code falls back to m.Timestamp (message arrival time). The grantedAt ordering comparison on line 488 ('grantedAt >= existing.grantedAt') thus compares message timestamps, not the explicitly provided granted_at. This is always correct in practice (messages are replayed in order), but the granted_at field intended for migration/import purposes is completely non-functional. Fix: use int64 unix nanoseconds in the payload, or parse RFC3339 in the struct.

payload.title: BUG: isHexString in inbox_watcher rejects uppercase hex pubkeys — valid join requests silently dropped

payload.context: pkg/conventionserver/inbox_watcher.go:217 — isHexString() only accepts lowercase hex [0-9a-f]. But join-request senders may encode their pubkey with uppercase hex [0-9A-F] (e.g., Go's encoding/hex produces lowercase, but other implementations may not). A valid 64-char pubkey with any uppercase character is rejected at line 121-123 with 'invalid pubkey format'. By contrast, isHex() in cmd/rd/join.go:410 accepts both cases. Fix: accept both cases in isHexString, or use strings.ToLower before validation.

payload.reason: Bug sweep complete: 2 critical, 4 high, 2 medium findings. Critical: (1) IssueToken panic on empty operations (ready-f34), (2) serverBindingPayload type mismatch breaks authorization gating (ready-3ef). High: (3) buildDepTree cycle detection broken allowing infinite recursion (ready-4b6), (4) validateNameFormat misses trailing /.. traversal (ready-bd0), (5) unguarded [:12] slice on message IDs panics if short (ready-6ef), (6) isHexString rejects uppercase pubkeys silently dropping valid join requests (ready-f32). Medium: (7) role-grant granted_at type mismatch — field silently ignored (ready-430).

payload.context: Antipattern: 58+ command handlers build convention args via argsMap := map[string]any — no type safety or validation. Should extract typed builders per operation (CreateArgs, ClaimArgs, etc.) to catch errors at build time and reduce boilerplate.

payload.reason: Fixed: added nil/empty check before operations slice access. Regression test added. PR: https://github.com/campfire-net/ready/pull/15

Fixed: cycle detection in buildDepTree now uses separate inPath map instead of deleting from visited. This prevents infinite recursion when nodes are reachable via multiple paths in a cyclic graph. Regression tests added for A↔B cycles and multi-path cycles. All tests passing. PR: https://github.com/campfire-net/ready/pull/16

Fixed: aligned serverBindingPayload ValidFrom/ValidUntil to int64 in state.go to match conventionserver. Replaced parseTimestamp() call sites with direct int64→nanoseconds conversion (p.ValidFrom * int64(time.Second)). Updated 5 existing fulfillment tests to use integer valid_from values. Added 3 regression tests in state_serverbinding_regression_test.go. All targeted tests pass. PR: https://github.com/campfire-net/ready/pull/17
